nodes.x: Bow down thine ear, O Lord, hear me: for I am poor and needy.
But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O Lord, make no tarrying.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
¶ Hear this, O ye that swallow up the needy, even to make the poor of the land to fail,
Preserve my soul; for I am holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.
The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.
Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.
Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
Spirit, will be saved ; for, says the Psalmist, “Thou shalt guide me with Thy counsel, and afterward receive me to* glory.”
Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, this is my gospel; and remember that they shall have faith in me or they can in nowise be saved;
Be merciful unto me, O Lord: for I cry unto thee daily.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.
Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
Yea, O God, and thou wast merciful unto me when I did cry unto thee in my field; when I did cry unto thee in my prayer, and thou didst hear me.
But unto thee have I cried, O Lord; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
Unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
Rejoice, O my heart, and cry unto the Lord, and say: O Lord, I will praise thee forever; yea, my soul will rejoice in thee, my God, and the rock of my salvation.

Lord, may my thoughts be turned to thee--
    Lift thou my heavy soul on high;
    Wilt thou, O Lord, return to me
    In mercy, Father, ere I die!
    My soaring thoughts now rise above--
    Oh fill my soul with heavenly love.
Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.
For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.

And forgive thy people that have sinned against thee, and all their transgressions wherein they have transgressed against thee, and give them compassion before them who carried them captive, that they may have compassion on them:

Therefore I say unto you, Go; and whosoever transgresseth against me, him shall ye judge according to the sins which he has committed; and if he confess his sins before thee and me, and repenteth in the sincerity of his heart, him shall ye forgive, and I will forgive him also.

May the eternal God make thy path straight, And the Lord [(be) with thee, and] protect thee from all evil, And grant unto thee grace, mercy and favour before those who see thee, And may none of the children of men have power over thee to harm thee; Go in peace.
But thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.

Therefore, my son, see that you are merciful unto your brethren; deal justly, judge righteously, and do good continually; and if ye do all these things then shall ye receive your reward; yea, ye shall have mercy restored unto you again; ye shall have justice restored unto you again; ye shall have a righteous judgment restored unto you again; and ye shall have good rewarded unto you again.
Give ear, O Lord, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
Give ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.
Hear the right, O Lord, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.

Hearken, O ye elders of my church, and give ear to the voice of the living God; and attend to the words of wisdom which shall be given unto you, according as ye have asked and are agreed as touching the church, and the spirits which have gone abroad in the earth.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, give ear to my supplications: in thy faithfulness answer me, and in thy righteousness.

1. Hearken, O ye Elders of my Church, and give ear to the voice
    of the living God, and attend to the words of wisdom which shall
    be given unto you, according as ye have asked and are agreed as
    touching the Church, and the spirits which have gone abroad in the
    earth.
In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.
And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.
Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
Though I walk in the midst of trouble, thou wilt revive me: thou shalt stretch forth thine hand against the wrath of mine enemies, and thy right hand shall save me.
Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord; neither are there any works like unto thy works.
O Lord, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
Wherefore thou art great, O Lord God: for there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
There is none holy as the Lord: for there is none beside thee: neither is there any rock like our God.
O Lord God, thou hast begun to shew thy servant thy greatness, and thy mighty hand: for what God is there in heaven or in earth, that can do according to thy works, and according to thy might?
But wilt thou know, O vain man, that faith without works is dead?
All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord; and shall glorify thy name.
Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for thou only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before thee; for thy judgments are made manifest.
And to make thee high above all nations which he hath made, in praise, and in name, and in honour; and that thou mayest be an holy people unto the Lord thy God, as he hath spoken.

And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee above measure, and make thy name great among all nations, and thou shalt be a blessing unto thy seed after thee, that in their hands they shall bear this ministry and Priesthood unto all nations;
O praise the Lord, all ye nations: praise him, all ye people.
For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
Thou art the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.
Thou shalt be perfect with the Lord thy God.
O Lord, for thy servant’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.
Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were created.
For thy word’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all these great things, to make thy servant know them.
Teach me thy way, O Lord; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.
Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
Lead me in thy truth, and teach me: for thou art the God of my salvation; on thee do I wait all the day.
Let those that fear thee turn unto me, and those that have known thy testimonies.
Teach me thy way, O Lord, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.
Shew me thy ways, O Lord; teach me thy paths.
I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
Every day will I bless thee; and I will praise thy name for ever and ever.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.
For great is thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest hell.
But do thou for me, O God the Lord, for thy name’s sake: because thy mercy is good, deliver thou me.
For thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
For thy mercy is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.
For thy mercy is great above the heavens: and thy truth reacheth unto the clouds.
O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.
The proud have forged a lie against me: but I will keep thy precepts with my whole heart.
For thou art the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
Every one that is proud shall be thrust through; yea, and every one that is joined to the wicked shall fall by the sword.

And all this that the innocent among you may not be condemned with the unjust; and that the guilty among you may not escape; because I, the Lord, have promised unto you a crown of glory at my right hand.
Arise, O Lord, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.
But thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.
For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
The Lord is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.
The Lord is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
O turn unto me, and have mercy upon me; give thy strength unto thy servant, and save the son of thine handmaid.
Now therefore, I pray thee, hearken thou also unto the voice of thine handmaid, and let me set a morsel of bread before thee; and eat, that thou mayest have strength, when thou goest on thy way.
Turn thee unto me, and have mercy upon me; for I am desolate and afflicted.
O Lord, truly I am thy servant; I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.

That this shall be no grief unto thee, nor offence of heart unto my lord, either that thou hast shed blood causeless, or that my lord hath avenged himself: but when the Lord shall have dealt well with my lord, then remember thine handmaid.
Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; and in thine hand is power and might; and in thine hand it is to make great, and to give strength unto all.
Shew me a token for good; that they which hate me may see it, and be ashamed: because thou, Lord, hast holpen me, and comforted me.

¶ Hear the word of the Lord, ye that tremble at his word; Your brethren that hated you, that cast you out for my name’s sake, said, Let the Lord be glorified: but he shall appear to your joy, and they shall be ashamed.
The Lord taketh my part with them that help me: therefore shall I see my desire upon them that hate me.
Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.
That thou mayest bear thine own shame, and mayest be confounded in all that thou hast done, in that thou art a comfort unto them.
But thou, O Lord, knowest me: thou hast seen me, and tried mine heart toward thee: pull them out like sheep for the slaughter, and prepare them for the day of slaughter.
